headline: MACC's 2025 conviction rate drops to 34%
context: MACC secured only 34% convictions in 2025, down from 52% in 2023, raising questions about prosecutorial effectiveness under Chief Commissioner Azam Baki's successor.

cards.big: Only 34% of MACC cases ended in conviction last year
cards.sub: Down from 52% in 2023 — nearly half of corruption prosecutions now fail

cards.big: Section 17A Corporate Liability Act saw zero convictions since 2020
cards.sub: Six years after enactment, no company has been found guilty under this provision

cards.big: MACC's RM380 million annual budget yields fewer results per ringgit
cards.sub: Cost per successful prosecution rose 47% compared to three years ago

cards.big: Prosecution division lost 12 senior DPPs to private sector in 2025
cards.sub: Brain drain weakens institutional capacity to handle complex financial crime cases

cards.big: Low conviction rates may signal weak case preparation, not less corruption
Should MACC have independent prosecutorial power separate from the AG?
